- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Project Borealis: Prologue

## System Information

- Video driver version: amdgpu kernel 6.11.6-arch1

- Proton version: 9.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2215490.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17707299/steam-2215490.log)

## Symptoms 
Game needs Visual Studio C++ Runtime to launch

## Reproduction
1. Install game
2. Click "Launch" and wait for the prefix to be created
3. Warning telling "Microsoft Visual C++ Runtime is needed" and the game closes
symptom_summary:
用户在将《帝国时代4》的语言设置为西班牙语后，游戏无法启动，出现黑屏，游戏鼠标可见，但随后关闭。 

Tags: Game Crash, Black Screen, Language Issue

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Zenith: The Last City
- Steam AppID of the game: 1403370

## System Information
- GPU: AMD 5700XT
- Driver/LLVM version: mesa 21.3.5-1
- Kernel version: 5.17.0-rc1
- [Link to full system information report](https://gist.github.com/duckbytes/0c2e538660d563c43320fcb6123b88dc)
- Proton version: Experimental

Log file:

[steam-1403370.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7967308/steam-1403370.zip)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms

Voice communication does not work. I cannot hear what people are saying. I don't know if other people can hear me talking.

When looking through settings it says "Vivox disabled". Click on the spanner icon on the far right, and the cog icon next to Voice Volume. Then look to the panel on the far right. Clicking Refresh VC doesn't help.

It does give me the option to set pulseaudio as my input and output so it seems to be detected.

I can hear all other sounds in the game.

## Reproduction

Start SteamVR. Start Zenith. Try to talk to other users. Check the options for Vivox status.

compatibility_report:
New World eventually hard crashes my machine and have to hard reboot.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: New World
- Steam AppID of the game: 1063730

## System Information
![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 19-10-31](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/5949142/b0261ac1-639d-47f3-ae73-0848aefe152b)
- [Gist](https://gist.github.com/mailhotz/11ae387d0e6ff4c82691828b4490962b)
- Proton Version: Experimental but have tried many different versions

# I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1063730.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13733763/steam-1063730.zip)

# Symptoms:
Game loads in fine, occasionally crashes after character select or sporadically during gameplay. It may be of note that my journal gets flooded with the following log line, on average it's posting the overrun log with different values about every 2-3ms
```
localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26846aaa0: [New World] overrun recover read:230400 avail:17408 max:15360 skip:13568
```
Example of frequency:
```
Dec 20 19:00:34 localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26846aaa0: [New World] overrun recover read:41568768 avail:28160 max:15360 skip:24320
Dec 20 19:00:35 localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26846aaa0: [New World] overrun recover read:41846528 avail:16640 max:15360 skip:12800
Dec 20 19:00:35 localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26a998b50: [New World] overrun recover read:40660480 avail:16896 max:15360 skip:13056
Dec 20 19:00:39 localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26a998b50: [New World] overrun recover read:42370816 avail:16640 max:15360 skip:12800
Dec 20 19:00:42 localhost.localdomain pipewire-pulse[2847]: mod.protocol-pulse: 0x55a26a998b50: [New World] overrun recover read:43474176 avail:17152 max:15360 skip:13312
```

Do not seem to have this log appear in any game besides New World.

[Game crash in journal after freezing machine](https://gist.github.com/mailhotz/c883e25f2ef618c3976fccd6f2b5bba0)

# Reproduction:
- Launch game from steam with `journalctl -f`
- Once selecting character and load into world
- Begin to see the above pipewire-pulse spew until game is closed or crashes OS

issue_title: Elder Scrolls Online, Zenimax Free Space Error / Directory Not Writable
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Elder Scrolls Online
- Steam AppID of the game: 306130

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1650
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 460.56
- Kernel version: 5.11.7-zen1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/jmgant/a1ebe8e180b4c61661994c9575d5646f
[steam-346110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6176252/steam-346110.log)

- Proton version: All
[steam-346110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6176226/steam-346110.log)


## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and drag
and drop the generated `$HOME/steam-$APPID.log` into this issue report -->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When I launch the game with Proton Experimental, the Zenimax installer says I only have 3GB available while I have over 1TB and won't install. When I launch with older Proton versions, the free space is reported correctly, however the Zenimax installer says the path to the game is not writable. I assure you it is, or has been made so.

I had this game on this system running at first. I removed it from an old, small drive, and it is only upon the reinstall that this has happened.



[steam-346110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6176234/steam-346110.log)

## Reproduction


<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in

-->
symptom_summary:
用户在使用Proton Experimental启动《Elder Scrolls Online》时，Zenimax安装程序显示可用空间仅为3GB，而实际上有超过1TB的可用空间，导致无法安装；使用旧版Proton时，虽然可用空间显示正确，但安装程序提示游戏路径不可写。 

Tags: Installation Issues, Disk Space Error, Path Writable Error

attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Game launched with only a black screen at first until I switched to Experimental. Afterwards game works even with default Proton but has serious performance issues. The second area drops well below ~15 fps on my hardware, likely unplayable on the Steam Deck - I will post an update once I get to try it there.

## Reproduction

Run game, maybe switch to Experimental if it only displays a black screen. Observe unexpectedly poor performance, especially visible while riding the stagecoach.

issue_title: Space Engineers (244850) crashes arbitrarily in faudio and SDL2
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Space Engineers
- Steam AppID of the game: 244850

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.3.4
- Kernel version: 5.16.1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/priand1/6c0059d4a5c3955e01dcd7b57f0af128):
- Proton version: 6.3

## I confirm:
- [X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)--> Proton-crash-log: [Gist](https://gist.github.com/priand1/a901217ca29c8167a2726056f5b566dd)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Space Engineers crashes arbitrarily. This can happen after 5 minutes of playing or after 5 hours.

Proton leaves crash reports in /tmp/proton_crashreports/ every time the game crashes.
According to these reports there seems to be a problem with faudio and/or SDL2

This happens with proton 6.3 as with proton experimental as well.

## Reproduction

Play SE.

#1792
symptom_summary:
用户在玩《太空工程师》时，游戏会随机崩溃，崩溃时间不定，可能在5分钟或5小时后发生。每次崩溃后，Proton会在/tmp/proton_crashreports/中留下崩溃报告，报告显示可能与faudio和/或SDL2有关。

**Tags:** Crashes, Random crashes, Faudio issues, SDL2 issues

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: [Drop Duchy](https://store.steampowered.com/app/2525310/Drop_Duchy/)
- Steam AppID of the game: 2525310

## System Information
- GPU: Nvidia GTX 1080Ti
- Video driver version: NVIDIA 570.144
- Kernel version: 6.14.6
- [Link to full system information report as Gist](https://gist.github.com/eloj/4409b6d7c59b558b244eff39118cb660).
- Proton version: 9.0, hotfix, experimental..

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The game "launches", but there's no window created nor any sound. The only option is to kill the process(es) using the Stop button. This seems a lot like #8682 though this game seems to use UE5.

## Reproduction

With my configuration, the reproduction is just to install the game and try to start it.

## Workaround

After trying all sorts of workarounds, including PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI, PROTON_USE_WINED3D, WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER -- without success -- I found a [post by the developer](https://steamcommunity.com/app/2525310/discussions/0/744882977527323256/) from where I learned that you can use the options '-force -dx11' to force it from DX12 to DX11, and this worked.

## Logs

In the default (non-working) case, the PROTON_LOG ends thus:

```
...
```
